title: Happy Dates
objectives: Identify key vocabulary related to Ramadan and national celebrations
Use present simple negative (don't / doesn't) correctly
Read and comprehend short passages about Eid Al-Fitr, Eid Al-Adha, and Prophet Muhammad's birthday
Use ordinal numbers and prepositions of time (in, on, at) with months and dates
Write short guided paragraphs about Ramadan and Kuwait's National Day

vocabulary.definition: A specific day of the month or year
To not eat or drink for a period of time, especially during Ramadan
Earlier than a particular time
The time of day when light first appears in the sky, before sunrise
Later than a particular time
The time when the sun goes down in the evening
Different from what is normal; important
One of the twelve periods of time that a year is divided into
To not remember something
To do something enjoyable for a special occasion
Words spoken to God; the act of praying
The parent of your mother or father
A mountain (e.g. Mount Arafat)
Having very little money; not rich
A special event or party for an important occasion
Relating to or belonging to a country
A period of 365 days (or 366 in a leap year)
To walk in a group with regular steps, often in a parade
Devices that explode with bright colours and loud noises in the sky
Having good things happen to you by chance
The day each year that is the same date as the one on which you were born
The occasion when a baby is born
To ask someone to come to a party or event
To organise or have an event
A day when you do not have to go to school or work
After some time; not now

key_concepts.explanation: Use 'don't' with I/you/we/they and 'doesn't' with he/she/it to make negative sentences. Examples: 'I don't drink water when I fast.' 'She doesn't stay up late in Ramadan.' After don't/doesn't, use the base form of the verb (not past tense).

Ramadan is a special month for Muslims. They fast from dawn to sunset — they don't eat or drink during the day. At sunset (iftar), they can eat. Suhoor is the meal before dawn. Common iftar foods include dates and soup.

Muslims celebrate Eid Al-Fitr after Ramadan and Eid Al-Adha on the 10th of Dhu Al-Hijjah. On Eid, people wear new clothes, go to the mosque for prayer, visit family and friends, and give gifts and money to children. For Eid Al-Adha, Muslims give lamb meat to family, friends, and poor people.
Kuwait's National Day is on the 25th of February. People celebrate with marching, discovering places, and watching fireworks. Al-Muhrah is a celebration where people visit Kuwait.

Ordinal numbers show position or order: 1st (first), 2nd (second), 3rd (third), 4th (fourth), etc. up to 12th (twelfth). Used with months: 'My birthday is on the 8th of December.' Prepositions of time: use 'on' for specific dates, 'in' for months/years/Ramadan.

On the 12th of Rabi' Al-Awwal, Muslims celebrate the birth of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH). Families visit each other and share sweets. It is a holiday. At school, teachers hold a celebration, give gifts, and tell stories about the Prophet's life. Students learn lessons like being kind and helping others.

examples.problem: Lesson 1 Listening: Listen to the recording about Ramadan and tick or cross
solution_steps: 1. Grandmother tells Noura to drink juice. (listen for this detail)
2. Noura's mother will make rice with chicken for suhoor. (listen for this detail)
3. Noura wants to fast for the first time. (listen for this detail)
examples.answer: Students listen to a recording about a family preparing for Ramadan and determine if statements are true or false. Then circle the food Noura ate for suhoor from pictures (dates, rice, chicken, laban, etc.).
examples.visual_description: Images of grandmother and Noura watching TV about Ramadan, and Noura with her mother at night. Food pictures: dates, rice, roast chicken, laban.

examples.problem: Lesson 3 Reading: Read about the Two Eids and answer
solution_steps: Read the passage about Eid Al-Fitr and Eid Al-Adha
Q1: The best title — a. Month of Ramadan, b. The Two Eids, c. Giving Gifts
Q2: The underlined word 'They' in line 4 refers to — a. people, b. grandparents, c. friends
Complete the timeline: Ramadan → Eid Al-Fitr → Dhu Al-Hijjah → Eid Al-Adha
examples.answer: 1. b. The Two Eids; 2. a. people; Timeline: Ramadan → Eid Al-Fitr → Dhu Al-Hijjah → Eid Al-Adha
examples.visual_description: Reading passage with illustrations of iftar meal, family in Eid clothes, Hajj pilgrimage, and a timeline graphic

examples.problem: Lesson 6 Grammar: Ordinal numbers and prepositions of time
solution_steps: Learn all 12 months with ordinal numbers: January=1st, February=2nd, ..., December=12th
Q1: My birthday is ............ the 8th of December. (a. in, b. on, c. at)
Q2: We fast ............ Ramadan. (a. in, b. on, c. at)
examples.answer: 1. b. on (use 'on' for specific dates); 2. a. in (use 'in' for months and Ramadan)
examples.visual_description: Calendar showing 12 months with ordinal numbers (1st through 12th), balloons, and children asking about birthdays

exercises.problem: She doesn't ............ up late in Ramadan.
The best title for the passage about the Two Eids could be:
The underlined word 'They' in line 4 refers to:
Write the missing words: 1. They __________ Eid by wearing new clothes. 2. Don't __________ to pray on time.
Write a paragraph about 'Ramadan' with the help of pictures and guide words: Muslims-fast, read-Quran, pray-mosque
exercises.answer: In Ramadan, Muslims fast from dawn to sunset. They read the Quran every day. They pray in the mosque. Ramadan is a special month.

exercises.problem: Al-Muhrah is from ............ (a. Kuwait, b. Bahrain, c. United Arab Emirates)
exercises.answer: Listen to determine the correct answer from the recording about Kuwait's National Day celebrations

exercises.problem: My birthday is ............ the 8th of December.
What do children learn from the story of our Prophet?
exercises.answer: They learn lessons like being kind and helping others.

exercises.problem: Write the missing words: 1. We celebrate Eid with our __________. 2. There are 365 days in a __________.
Write a paragraph about 'Kuwait's National Day' with guide words: march-street, discover-places, watch-fireworks
exercises.answer: On Kuwait's National Day, we march in the street. We discover new places. We watch fireworks at night. It is a happy celebration.

exercises.problem: Read, then tick: Is 'December' a Day, month, or year? Is '2028' a Day, month, or year?

teaching_notes: This unit covers Ramadan, the two Eids, Kuwait's National Day, and Prophet Muhammad's birthday — all culturally significant for Kuwaiti students. Grammar focus is present simple negative (don't/doesn't) and ordinal numbers with prepositions of time (in/on/at). The unit has 8 sub-lessons alternating between listening, grammar, reading, and spelling/writing for two themes. Use the song 'We Don't Eat Before Dawn' to reinforce don't/doesn't. Connect to students' personal experiences: ask about their Ramadan routines, Eid celebrations, and birthdays. The vocabulary list has 26 words across all 8 sub-lessons.
